questions.question: What does the Sampler primitive return?
questions.explanation: Sampler runs circuits with measurements and returns quasi-probability distributions of outcomes, similar to running on real hardware.

questions.question: What does the Estimator primitive compute?
questions.explanation: Estimator calculates expectation values ⟨ψ|O|ψ⟩ for given observables O, useful for VQE and other variational algorithms.

questions.question: What is the memory scaling of statevector simulation for n qubits?
questions.explanation: Statevector requires storing 2^n complex amplitudes, making it impractical for more than ~30 qubits.

questions.question: What is a depolarizing error?

options.text: Randomly applies I, X, Y, or Z with some probability

questions.explanation: Depolarizing error randomly applies one of the Pauli operators (including identity) with specified probability, modeling symmetric noise.

questions.question: Which simulation method can handle ~1000 qubits?
questions.explanation: Clifford simulators can efficiently simulate circuits containing only Clifford gates (H, S, CNOT) even for thousands of qubits.

questions.question: When would you use density matrix simulation?

options.text: For simulating noise and mixed states

questions.explanation: Density matrix simulation can represent mixed states and noise effects, though it scales as O(4^n) limiting it to ~15 qubits.

questions.question: Statevector simulation requires the circuit to have measurements.
questions.explanation: False. Statevector simulation gives the exact quantum state without measurements. Measurements collapse the state and are typically omitted for statevector analysis.
